title: collinsonia canadensis var. punctata
intro: Collinsonia canadensis var. punctata, also known as spotted horseweed, is a perennial plant in the mint family native to North America. It has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to soothe digestive issues, reduce fever, and treat wounds. The plant is also believed to have health benefits such as reducing stress, improving circulation, and supporting respiratory health. Collinsonia canadensis var. punctata can be prepared in herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, and its leaves and roots can be used to create salves and other topical preparations.
